text: 3408 - وَ- سَأَلَ أَبُو أَيُّوبَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُحِيلُ الرَّجُلَ بِالْمَالِ أَ يَرْجِعُ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ لَا يَرْجِعُ عَلَيْهِ أَبَداً إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدْ أَفْلَسَ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ‌.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3408 - Abu Ayyub asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who transfers a debt to another man (through a payment order or promissory note). Does the original debtor still remain liable for the payment?
Imam (as) replied: "No, he does not return to him (for payment) at all, except if the one to whom the debt was transferred had already declared bankruptcy before the transfer."
